kept.text:
Funfact:

Industri perfilman dan K-Drama kejatuhannya justru karena Netflix. Kenapa?

Karena Netflix numpahin milyaran US dollar buat filmmakers Korea. Pasti investor pengen balik modal dong? Wajar emang. Tapi...

Ini bikin filmmaker jadi gak berani ambil risiko. Penulisan dan konsep yang dibikin cuma ngikutin data dan formula yang terbukti sukses. Makanya terkesan "gitu-gitu aja".

Malah makin sedikit yang berani bikin breakthrough dalam konteks perfilman.

Sekarang industri Drachin yang low budget yang ternyata disukai Asia Tenggara wkwk.

Selengkapnya:

https://t.co/48T0oitvo7

profile_bio.description: I talk about popculture and its worldbuilding. Also book review

value.string_value: 29K likes, 3.4K comments. "How Netflix Ruined Korean Dramas Forever | AB Explained"

profile_bio.description: like & subscribe

quoted_tweet.text:
The Golden age of Korean pop and Dramas is pretty much ending in most of Southeast Asia. 

I don’t think this is because Korean products have gotten weaker though, I think it’s because of a process I’ll call Netflixzation. 

Netflix kind of diffused the best filmmaking practices and concepts from America globally in a rush of tens of billions of dollars, this means a lot of local film industries that would have been slower to develop, got a huge jolt all at once, and local audiences grew more comfortable consuming their own local products. 

The same has happened via pop music where a lot of the best learnings around music production + factory systems easily flowed into Southeast Asia as well. 

There are just far more excellent cultural products emerging all through Asia, and as a result there’s greater fragmentation into local scenes. 

This will likely happen within Africa in a decade too as it gets richer, Nollywood and Nigerian dominated Afrobeats won’t reign forever.

profile_bio.description: An American Living The Asian Century. Also Living The AI Century. Get Rich AF.

kept.text:
[INFO] 260421 Netflix “When Life Gives You Tangerines” starring IU continues to make lasting Tourism and Economic Impact on Jeju Island, South Korea

@Variety: The cultural spillover into tourism is quantified in the report. 38.3% of inbound tourists said they were motivated to visit Korea after engaging with Korean Wave content, up from 32.1% a year earlier – the most frequently cited reason for visiting the country. A case study on the 2025 Netflix K-drama “When Life Gives You Tangerines,” set in Jeju’s fishing villages, illustrates the mechanism directly: after the series topped global non-English rankings, Jeju posted year-on-year foreign visitor growth every month from April, with January–September arrivals reaching 1.74 million, up 17.5%. The Jeju Haenyeo Museum, featured prominently in the series, saw foreign visits climb 58.9% to nearly 50,000 by November.

https://t.co/wwA5T1Phox
#IU #아이유 #폭싹속았수다 #WhenLifeGivesYouTangerines

kept.text:
CONTENT OVER STARDOM 🔥🔥

In the first 10 days on Netflix:
#Youth leads with 4.5M views, ahead of star driven films like #ThugLife (4.4M), #VidaaMuyarchi (4.3M) and #Indian2 (4.2M).

Yet OTT platforms pay hundreds of crores for star led films, while smaller non star films often outperform them. This isn’t limited to one industry, it’s happening everywhere. Producers need to prioritise strong content over blindly relying on stardom!!

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
ファッションデザイナー／クリエイティブディレクター 
落合宏理の #ネトフリこれ見て 
┗━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

◆ウェンズデー
『彼女の意志の強い言動に宿る優しさが心に響く』

15歳の頃にティム・バートンに出会って、アウトサイダーの孤独と悲しい目つきが美しいものだと知りました。特に印象に残っているのは、シーズン1で、ウェンズデーがイーニッドを抱きしめるシーン。拒否しながらも、その後、強く抱きしめ返す。あの瞬間、彼女の心が確かに動いたのが伝わってくる。ウェンズデーは孤独に対する誇りとどこか寂しさが交わっている。だからこそ、彼女の意志の強い言動には優しさがあり、心に響くんだと思う。

◆落合宏理（ファッションデザイナー／クリエイティブディレクター）
1977年生まれ。アパレルテキスタイルの会社を経て2007年にFACETASMを設立。2016年に毎日ファッション大賞の「大賞」を受賞。個人としては2021年よりコンビニエンスストア、Family Martとの協業でConvenience Wearを発表している。2025年より常石グループのチーフ・デザイン・オフィサーに就任。

kept.text:
いいか？GWをどう過ごすかで今後の人生が変わると言っても過言じゃない。ダメなヤツの特徴が２つある。1つ目は日中にNetflixを見る奴だ。夜見れば良いものを、太陽の日が出てるときに一気見する者はナンセンスだ。2つ目は1日の半分以上をベッドで過ごす者。これは論外だ。両方に当てはまるヤツ手を

挙げてみろ。
